And that makes perfect sense. It's incongruent.
So let me catch you up and explain.

expensive stuff ...for a 98.83% discount.

weird.
So here’s why I’m doing it.
I’m filming a training on email marketing for some of my clients. And I wanted to show them how
to take IMPERFECT action and still increase revenue ...just by making a good offer.
So I chose THIS offer to use as an example. ←- 98.83% discount
The reason why I chose it is because the “sales video” is HORRIBLE. (It’s under three minutes
and it was broken for most of the past two days.)
No really - it’s bad. No hook, no benefits - just a really quick explanation of what you get with
this crazy discount.
See it here.
Anyway, I don’t suggest people deliberately use bad pitches lol. The reason I chose this one is
that it was already made ...and the “rules” are that I had to create this whole promotion ...without
creating any new stuff.
(The point of the lesson is to demonstrate how much potential profit people are leaving on the
table by not making offers).
So that’s the “backstory”.
